name: oc22

data_origin.blend: DFT relaxations with oxide-appropriate exchange-correlation functional (different from OC20's PBE)

env_axis.name: composition-class
env_axis.shift_mechanism: oxide catalysts vs OC20's metal catalysts; also different DFT functional

ood_protocol.name: oc20-to-oc22-cross-composition
ood_protocol.ref: Tran et al. 2023 ACS Catalysis (OC22 dataset paper)

facts: 62k oxide-catalyst DFT relaxations; different exchange-correlation functional than OC20.
hypotheses: Method ordering on OC20 does not preserve under OC20→OC22 transfer: the functional change re-ranks methods.
'Oxide vs metal' is a phase-space shift requiring a new primitive rather than a plain compositional shift.
intuitions: The OC22 paper likely reports its own cross-OC20 baseline numbers, possibly updated by the 2024-25 literature.
Oxide-catalyst-specific transfer studies using experimental ground truth probably exist.
unknowns: Whether the right env unit is the pair (OC20-train, OC22-test) or a joint env factor (functional × composition) cannot be settled: the pairing tacitly assumes the former, and testing that assumption requires the pair, which is circular.
epistemic_state.boundary: Same simulator caveat as OC20. Pair-only value: OC22 alone has too few samples for compositional analysis without OC20 anchoring.

representational_commitments: Single-cation oxides only — multi-cation systems excluded
materialization_status: Full OC22 (57 GB) — s2ef_total LMDBs (20 GB) + is2res LMDBs (114 MB) + trajectories (34 GB) + metadata + oc20_ref. URL pattern dl.fbaipublicfiles.com/opencatalystproject/data/oc22/<file> (the missing /oc22/ subdirectory in earlier attempts was the issue).
